mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample multivariate testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenue forecasts and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are negative when both series grow concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations during tightening periods, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO changes falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated while year-over-year CPI exceeds 3.0 percent, the combination of high borrowing costs and persistent inflation compresses corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where both conditions hold, or the interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates severe economic deceleration that forces equity markets to price in recession risks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of low-growth quarters or if the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust labor market expansion that supports consumer income and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces retail sales growth, manufacturers accumulate inventories that signal upcoming capacity utilization improvements, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
